name: Aranjuez and Real Cortijo de San Isidro
name_original: Aranjuez y Real Cortijo de San Isidro desde Ocaña
description: Cycle 29.7 miles from Ocaña to explore the Royal Palace of Aranjuez and Real Cortijo de San Isidro, a moderate route.
region_intro: Road cycling around Ocaña is characterized by its generally flat terrain, situated on the Mesa de Ocaña at an elevation of about 2,400 feet (730 meters). The region features open plains and agricultural landscapes, with routes often following river valleys such as the Tagus, Martín Román, and Algodor. This environment provides opportunities for long-distance rides with minimal elevation changes. Best road cycling routes around Ocaña The most popular road cycling route is Yepes y Huerta de Valdecarabanos – circular from Ocaña, a 30.3 miles (48.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a circular path through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Aranjuez and Yepes - circular from Ocaña, a moderate 43.8 miles (70.4 km) path. This route connects Ocaña with the historic town of Aranjuez and the village of Yepes. Local road cyclists also love the Aranjuez and Real Cortijo de San Isidro from Ocaña, a 29.7 miles (47.8 km) trail leading through the plains towards Aranjuez, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Ocaña is defined by flat plains, agricultural land, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more moderate distances. The routes in Ocaña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ocaña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Ocaña?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available around Ocaña, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. The komoot community has explored 13 routes in total, with options ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Ocaña?
A: Road cycling around Ocaña is characterized by its generally flat terrain, situated on the Mesa de Ocaña. You'll find open plains and agricultural landscapes, with many routes following river valleys such as the Tagus, Martín Román, and Algodor. This environment is ideal for long-distance rides with minimal elevation changes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Ocaña offers routes suitable for beginners. For an easy ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025395667/yepes-y-huerta-de-valdecarabanos-circular-from-ocana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yepes y Huerta de Valdecarabanos – circular from Ocaña&lt;/a&gt;, a 30.3-mile (48.7 km) trail that provides a circular path through the local countryside with minimal elevation.

Q: Can I find longer, more challenging road cycling routes in Ocaña?
A: While the terrain is generally flat, you can find moderate routes that cover longer distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025349836/aranjuez-and-yepes-circular-from-ocana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranjuez and Yepes - circular from Ocaña&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 43.8-mile (70.4 km) path that connects Ocaña with the historic town of Aranjuez and the village of Yepes, offering a good distance challenge.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Ocaña?
A: Yes, many routes around Ocaña are designed as circular loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31830537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez – Royal Palace of Aranjuez loop from Estación de Ocaña&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 65.6 km (40.8 miles) route that allows you to start and end in the same location, passing by significant landmarks.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Ocaña?
A: While road cycling around Ocaña, you can encounter several natural monuments and features. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5225122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Seca de las Esteras&lt;/a&gt;, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardín del Príncipe&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/407021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinescos Pond&lt;/a&gt;. These offer serene spots and scenic views along your ride.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest accessible by bike?
A: Absolutely. Many routes will take you past or near significant historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025345909/aranjuez-and-real-cortijo-de-san-isidro-from-ocana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranjuez and Real Cortijo de San Isidro from Ocaña&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, leads towards Aranjuez, where you can find the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2780461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Cortijo of San Isidro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Ocaña?
A: Ocaña, Spain, experiences a Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild winters. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak summer heat while still enjoying good weather conditions.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that pass through towns with amenities like cafes?
A: Yes, several routes connect Ocaña with nearby towns, providing opportunities for breaks and refreshments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025349836/aranjuez-and-yepes-circular-from-ocana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranjuez and Yepes - circular from Ocaña&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, passes through the historic town of Aranjuez and the village of Yepes, where you can find cafes and other amenities.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Ocaña?
A: The road cycling routes in Ocaña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the flat, open terrain which is ideal for steady, long-distance rides, and the scenic agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically highlight the town of Ocaña itself?
A: Yes, you can explore routes that focus on the immediate surroundings of Ocaña. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31828279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Ocaña loop from Estación de Ocaña&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 63 km (39.1 miles) route that allows you to experience the local area, including the historic Plaza Mayor.
tips:
Aranjuez, located in the Community of Madrid, is a Royal Site and Villa with a rich history and beauty.
Royal Palace of Aranjuez: This majestic residence of the Spanish royal family is located on the banks of the Tagus River. Its gardens, such as those of the Island, Isabel II and the Jardín del Príncipe, are aesthetic spaces and plant museums. The Prince's Garden houses the Fountain of Apollo and the Asian and American Islands.

This old agricultural farmhouse, ordered to be created in 1766 by Charles III, sits on land that previously belonged to the 12th century town of Villafranca. With an area of 1,126 hectares, it is located about six kilometers northeast of Aranjuez, on the Colmenar de Oreja highway (M-305).

The M-305 highway in Aranjuez passes by the Puente de la Reina (Queen's Bridge), a key point for accessing the Royal Estate of San Isidro and connecting with the Prince's Garden.

From this point, Calle San Isidro, lined with linden and elm trees, leads directly to the Royal Estate of San Isidro.
It is a paved secondary road with two lanes, connecting Aranjuez with Colmenar de Oreja and other nearby towns.
It forms part of the southern access to the Royal Site of Aranjuez and is surrounded by historical and agricultural areas.

The Plaza Mayor of Ocaña (Toledo) is considered one of the most impressive in Castilla-La Mancha due to its size, harmony, and historical significance.

It is an arcaded square in the Baroque and Neoclassical styles, begun in 1777 by order of Charles III to emulate the Plaza Mayor of Madrid, albeit on a smaller scale.

Its dimensions are imposing: 55 x 52 meters, almost square, with 70 stone pillars and semicircular arches supporting balconies and dormer windows.
Symmetry and proportion are highly valued, creating an elegant and monumental visual effect.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 47.8 km (about 29.7 miles) long. Komoot estimates the cycling duration to be around 2 hours and 22 minutes, but this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to explore the sights.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this as a 'moderate' road ride, meaning it requires good fitness. While the majority is well-paved, some segments might be unpaved and more challenging. It's not ideal for absolute beginners but suitable for those with some cycling experience and a good fitness level.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling route in Ocaña?
faqs.answer: The route starts in Ocaña, which offers historical highlights like its Plaza Mayor of Ocaña. You should be able to find public parking within Ocaña, close to the starting point of the route. Specific parking details are not provided, so checking local parking options upon arrival is recommended.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of well-paved surfaces, with about 38.0 km of general road and 9.07 km of street. There's also a small segment of state road. While mostly smooth, be prepared for some potentially unpaved sections that could add to the challenge. The elevation gain is around 340 meters (1,115 feet).

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy a scenic journey through historical and agricultural landscapes. Key highlights include the majestic Royal Palace of Aranjuez and its elaborate gardens, the historical Royal Cortijo of San Isidro with its unique pentagonal design, and charming Tree-Lined Roads to Aranjuez. The route also passes the Plaza Mayor of Ocaña.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the historical sites and agricultural scenery comfortably, spring and autumn are generally the best times. The weather is usually milder, making for a more pleasant ride. Summers can be very hot in this region, and winters can be chilly.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino Natural del Tajo (23% of the route), MTB Madrid a Aranjuez (14%), and Paseo Calle de la Reina (9%). You'll also encounter parts of Carril Bici (ciclo-turista) Andrés Oliva and various local streets.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows public roads and paths, specific regulations for dogs on bikes are not detailed. If you plan to visit historical sites like the Royal Palace or Cortijo, check their individual pet policies. Generally, dogs on a leash are tolerated in public outdoor spaces, but always be mindful of local rules and other cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially within the ZEPA Carrizales y Sotos de Aranjuez?
faqs.answer: No specific cycling permits are required for the route itself. However, the trail passes through the ZEPA Carrizales y Sotos de Aranjuez, which is a protected natural area. While there are no entrance fees for cycling through, it's important to respect environmental regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife. Entrance fees may apply if you choose to visit specific attractions like the Royal Palace of Aranjuez.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Ocaña and Aranjuez offer various options for food and drink. Aranjuez, being a Royal Site, has numerous cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel. The Royal Cortijo of San Isidro also has historical significance related to wine production, and you might find local establishments offering refreshments there.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given it's a moderate road cycling route, wear appropriate cycling attire, including a helmet. Bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months, and some snacks. A basic repair kit for punctures is also advisable. Don't forget your camera to capture the beautiful scenery and historical landmarks!
